description: The Obshchak is a traditional umbrella organization of criminal groups in Estonia, functioning as a trade union that settles conflicts and establishes boundaries among groups like the Russian mafia and Estonian organized crime. It pays for lawyers, delivers packages to imprisoned members, and covers other expenses. Dominated by Nikolai Tarankov from 2003 until his murder in 2016, the organization has about ten member groups and has been involved in drug trafficking, tax fraud, and violent crimes. Estonian police have disrupted its operations by arresting high-ranking members since the 2010s.

country_links.evidence_quote: The members have also operated significantly in Finland, where in 2009, high-level drug dealing was controlled by Estonians.
country_links.context: The organization's members have operated significantly in Finland, where they controlled high-level drug dealing.
